title: Anesthesia and Respiration
publisher: American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS)
abstract:
When
            Laminaria
            is exposed to the action of anesthetics (in sufficient concentration to produce any result) there is an increase in respiration. This may be followed by a decrease if the reagent is sufficiently toxic. No decrease is observed with low concentrations which are not toxic.
          
          These facts contradict the theory of Verworn that anesthesia is a kind of asphyxia, for his view is based upon the assumption that anesthetics decrease respiration.
